title: Drowning Rescue
immediate_action: Call emergency services. Do NOT enter water unless trained. Reach or throw something.

steps.instruction: Call emergency services. Do NOT jump in unless you are a trained swimmer — drowning victims can pull rescuers under.
steps.tip: Reach with a pole, branch, or towel. Throw a flotation device, ball, or container.

steps.instruction: Once out of water: check for responsiveness and breathing. If not breathing, begin rescue breaths immediately — give 5 initial breaths.
steps.tip: Drowning is an airway problem. Rescue breaths are critical.

steps.instruction: If no pulse after rescue breaths, begin full CPR: 30 compressions + 2 breaths.
steps.tip: Do not waste time trying to drain water from lungs — start CPR immediately.

steps.instruction: Continue CPR until emergency services arrive. Drowning victims can recover even after prolonged submersion — do NOT give up.
steps.tip: Cold water submersion victims have survived after 30+ minutes. Keep going.

steps.instruction: Once breathing resumes, place in recovery position. Keep warm — remove wet clothing and cover with blankets.
steps.tip: Monitor closely — secondary drowning can occur hours later. Hospital visit is mandatory.

do_not: Do NOT enter water unless trained — use reach/throw methods
Do NOT try to drain water from lungs
Do NOT assume recovery — always go to hospital
